expected_answer.root_cause: A Prometheus recording rule drops the 'condition' label during aggregation, causing the derived metric to always evaluate to 1. This triggers a perpetually firing NodeNotReady alert, which matches Alertmanager's inhibition rule and suppresses all critical alerts including DiskUsageCritical.
error_chain: Recording rule 'node:ready:condition' aggregates kube_node_status_condition but drops 'condition' label
Prometheus rules/manager.go evaluates the recording rule, producing a metric that always equals 1
Alert rule NodeNotReady fires based on the incorrect recording rule value
Prometheus sends NodeNotReady alert to Alertmanager
Alertmanager inhibit/inhibitor.go matches NodeNotReady as source alert against inhibition rule
Inhibition rule suppresses DiskUsageCritical because both have severity=critical
DiskUsageCritical alert is never dispatched to notification receivers
affected_components: All critical-severity alerts that share the inhibition rule target matcher
expected_answer.remediation: Fix the recording rule to preserve the 'condition' label during aggregation, or scope the inhibition rule more narrowly to prevent a single source alert from suppressing unrelated target alerts. Add alerting on inhibition rule match counts to detect over-broad suppression.
